name: Bizhan
description: Bizhan, a prominent figure in Persian mythology and the epic poem Shahnameh (Book of Kings) by Ferdowsi, is renowned for his valor, loyalty, and occasional rashness. He is the son of Giv, a celebrated warrior of the Kayanian dynasty, and Banu Goshasp, the daughter of Rostam, the greatest hero of Persian lore, making him a grandson of Rostam. Bizhan inherits the strength and courage of his lineage, displaying exceptional martial prowess from a young age. He is a loyal companion and a fierce warrior in the service of the Kayanian kings. His most famous exploit is his ill-fated encounter with Manizheh, the daughter of Afrasiab, the king of Turan, Persia's eternal rival. Lured by her beauty, Bizhan ventures into Turan, where he is captured and imprisoned by Afrasiab. Rostam, upon learning of Bizhan's plight, undertakes a perilous journey into Turan to rescue his grandson, demonstrating the deep familial bonds and the importance of honor in Persian culture. Bizhan's story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of impulsive decisions and the importance of loyalty, while also highlighting the unwavering commitment of family members to each other. He embodies the ideals of Persian heroism, showcasing bravery, strength, and a willingness to sacrifice for his king and his kin. Though sometimes prone to recklessness, Bizhan's courage and loyalty ultimately redeem him, solidifying his place as a significant hero within Persian mythology.
subtitle: Valiant warrior, grandson of Rostam.
achievements: Participated in numerous battles for the Kayanian kings.
Inspired Rostam to undertake a rescue mission.
Demonstrated unwavering loyalty to his king.
Proved his courage in the face of adversity.

keyMyths.title: Bizhan and Manizheh
keyMyths.description: Bizhan is lured into Turan by Manizheh's beauty and is subsequently imprisoned by Afrasiab. This event leads to Rostam's intervention and rescue mission.

keyMyths.title: Bizhan's Capture
keyMyths.description: Bizhan's capture in Turan highlights the dangers of impulsive decisions and the conflict between Persia and Turan. It showcases the importance of loyalty and the lengths to which heroes will go to protect their kin.

keyMyths.title: Rostam's Rescue of Bizhan
keyMyths.description: Rostam's rescue of Bizhan exemplifies the strength of familial bonds and the unwavering commitment to honor and duty. It is a testament to Rostam's legendary status and his love for his grandson.

sources.author: Ferdowsi
sources.description: The primary source for Bizhan's story and Persian mythology in general.
sources.text: The primary source for Bizhan's story and Persian mythology in general.
